_notes: Phase 2 watched-source expansion. Every entry is intentionally disabled (enabled: false) and verify_before_enabling: true. URLs default to canonical HighLevel domain roots — section-specific URLs under help.gohighlevel.com will be refined during verification (the help center URL pattern cannot be enumerated without fetching, and live fetching is disabled in this phase). Section-level watching first; article-level watching is a later phase, gated on the fetcher being implemented. Only official HighLevel domains belong here. See README.md > 'Watched-source expansion strategy' and docs/implementation-guide.md > '7. Phase 2: Watched-source expansion'.

schema.label: Human-readable description. Required.
schema.source_type: One of: api_docs | help_center | changelog | developer_portal | other. Required.
schema.expected_content_type: One of: html | json | openapi | markdown. Required. Drives how the parser should diff this source.
schema.priority: One of: high | medium | low. Required. Drives downstream alerting and review urgency.
schema.enabled: Boolean. Set to true only after URL has been verified and the fetcher is implemented.
schema.verify_before_enabling: Boolean. Keep true until the URL has been confirmed current, official, and granular enough.
schema.content_selector: CSS or XPath selector that isolates the meaningful content (drops nav/footer/sidebar). Null is allowed for section-level watching during Phase 2; refine when moving to article-level watching or when whole-page diffing is too noisy.
schema.owner: GitHub username responsible for reviewing changes from this source. Null is allowed during scaffolding.
schema.phase: One of: 1-critical | 2-expansion. Drives rollout order — Phase 1 sources are verified and enabled first.
schema.surfaces: Array of high-level product surfaces this source feeds (api, workflows, ai-agents, conversations, calendars, phone, messaging, email, forms, payments, crm, sites, reputation, integrations, settings, reporting, saas, general). Drives routing into surface-specific implication docs.

pages.label: HighLevel API Documentation (Stoplight) — root
pages.notes: Root of HighLevel public API documentation. When moving past section-level watching, prefer per-resource pages and the OpenAPI spec (JSON) for structured diffing.

pages.label: HighLevel Help Center — root
pages.notes: Root of HighLevel public help center. Top-level entry; specific section URLs in other entries should be refined to point at their own landing pages during verification.

pages.label: HighLevel Platform Updates / What's Changed
pages.notes: Platform changelog / 'What's Changed' feed. Canonical URL to be confirmed during verification (likely a dedicated path under help.gohighlevel.com or a separate changelog subdomain). Root used as placeholder until confirmed.
